fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

top_risks: High one-to-many ratios observed directly in correspondence CSVs
High missingness in fused attributes (coverage gaps)

findings.title: High one-to-many ratios observed directly in correspondence CSVs
findings.details: Correspondence edges suggest ambiguous matching (many-to-many tendencies), which can cause over-clustering and noisy fusion.
findings.recommendation: Increase match confidence threshold, add tie-breaking features (year/platform/developer), and consider enforcing 1-1 assignment for certain sources where appropriate.

findings.title: Possible noisy list fusion (concatenated values)
findings.details: Some string columns appear to contain delimiter-separated lists, which can indicate naive concatenation during fusion rather than true normalization/deduplication.
findings.recommendation: For multi-valued fields, normalize into sets with canonicalization and deduplication (casefold/trim), or choose a single best value by source priority rather than concatenation.

findings.title: High missingness in fused attributes (coverage gaps)
findings.details: Several columns are mostly null in the fused output, suggesting incomplete coverage or failed value propagation.
findings.recommendation: Review schema matching and fusion rules for these attributes; verify that source columns are mapped and that null-handling prefers non-null values.

recommendations: Increase match confidence threshold, add tie-breaking features (year/platform/developer), and consider enforcing 1-1 assignment for certain sources where appropriate.
Review schema matching and fusion rules for these attributes; verify that source columns are mapped and that null-handling prefers non-null values.
For multi-valued fields, normalize into sets with canonicalization and deduplication (casefold/trim), or choose a single best value by source priority rather than concatenation.
Add run-to-run regression checks: cluster health (max cluster size, one-to-many ratio), fused size drift, and attribute null rates.
Emit traceability fields (source id, source dataset, match confidence) in fused output to make debugging integration errors actionable.

notes: fused_review_table.csv includes a small set of key fused columns plus source coverage signals.
source_lineage_long.csv is attribute-long; it only includes attributes that overlap between fused and each source dataset (case-sensitive, then case-insensitive).
fusion_vs_testset_diff.csv compares aligned testset records to fused output when the testset XML is available; otherwise it is empty.

warnings: Dataset paths not found in context JSON; discovered CSVs in output/schema-matching/

Bases:
BasePostClusterer
Finds optimal one-to-one matching using maximum bipartite matching.
This algorithm formulates the entity matching problem as a maximum weight
bipartite matching problem and finds the globally optimal solution using
the Hungarian algorithm or similar optimization methods.
Unlike the greedy approach, this guarantees the optimal solution that
maximizes the total similarity score while enforcing one-to-one constraints.
Requires NetworkX for the bipartite matching implementation.

class

(
BasePostClusterer
):
"""Finds optimal one-to-one matching using maximum bipartite matching.
This algorithm formulates the entity matching problem as a maximum weight
bipartite matching problem and finds the globally optimal solution using
the Hungarian algorithm or similar optimization methods.
Unlike the greedy approach, this guarantees the optimal solution that
maximizes the total similarity score while enforcing one-to-one constraints.

Source code for PyDI.informationextraction.evaluation
"""Evaluation framework for information extraction in PyDI.
This module provides an evaluator to compare extracted (predicted)
attributes against an expected/validation dataset with common IR-style metrics.
It supports per-attribute evaluation functions (e.g., exact match,
tokenized string match, numeric tolerance) and produces both attribute-level
and dataset-level (micro/macro) metrics, including precision, recall, F1, and
non-null accuracy.
"""
from
__future__
import
annotations
from
typing
import
Any
,
Callable
,
Dict
,
List
,
Optional
,
Tuple
,
Union
from
pathlib
import
Path
import
json
import
logging
import
pandas
as
pd
# Reuse flexible evaluation functions from the fusion module
from
PyDI.fusion.evaluation
import
(
exact_match
,
tokenized_match
,
numeric_tolerance_match
,
set_equality_match
,
boolean_match
,
year_only_match
,
)
logger
=
logging
.
getLogger
(
__name__
)
